question:

$\textbf{(A)}\ 1 \qquad\textbf{(B)}\ 3\qquad\textbf{(C)}\ 12\qquad\textbf{(D)}\ 1239\qquad\textbf{(E)}\ 1265$

Since the sum of the digits of $n$ is 1274, which is much larger than 9 (the maximum sum of digits for a single digit number), there is a high probability that the sum of the digits of $n+1$ will be 1274, unless a carry-over increases the sum to 1275. We will check the possible values again considering the sum 1274 and the nature of digit sums under carry-over. </think> <answer> 12 </answer>
